GENERATION xG - MON 26 MAY (Live)
programme.desc: A deep dive into the tactical trends and statistical insights of modern football with input from players, managers and data experts from the 'fanalyst' community. This programme will analyse the on-field action in forensic detail and give hard core Premier League fans a new insight into what is happening on the pitch and why.

programme.title: FANTASY SHOW - MON 26 MAY
programme.desc: Saka or Salah? Foden or Fernandes? Wilson or Watkins? Picking your Fantasy team is never easy but this season the Fantasy Show is on hand to help you with those big decisions. Fantasy experts will crunch the numbers and give their opinion on which Premier League stars you should be selecting ahead of each FPL Gameweek.

programme.title: NETBUSTERS
programme.desc: Relive the best action from the Premier League with this fast-paced ISO angle highlights show, featuring every game and every goal from each matchround.

programme.title: FANZONE - MON 26 MAY
programme.desc: This is the show that puts international fans at the heart of the Premier League conversation. A phone-in for the digital generation, fans from around the world join our studio discussion via camera phones and weblinks to have their say about their teams and their favourite players.

programme.title: KELLY & WRIGHTY - MON 26 MAY
programme.desc: Michael Owen and Darren Lewis are in the studio alongside Kelly Cates and Ian Wright to reflect on the end of the 2024/25 Premier League season.

programme.title: PL STORIES

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football-Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United's record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides-Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he's become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp.

programme.title: PL MASTERCLASS
programme.desc: Some of the Premier League's biggest stars take us onto the pitch to discuss the techniques and thought processes that allow them to perform at the highest level. Featuring Jan Paul Van Hecke, Leicester City and Brentford goalscorers and West Ham's Thomas Soucek.

programme.title: PL STORIES

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We take a look at the career of Philippe Coutinho, tracing his journey from a promising young talent to one of the Premier League's most skilful and dynamic players. We join Coutinho at his current club Vasco da Gama and reflect on his time in Europe, from his rise at Liverpool to his time at Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Aston Villa. Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.title: Wolverhampton Wanderers vs Brentford (MW38)
programme.desc: Brentford have scored 65 Premier League goals this season, their most in a top-flight campaign since 1937-38 (69). Their conversion rate in 2024-25 is 15.2%, the best of any side.

programme.title: Ipswich Town vs West Ham United (MW38)
programme.desc: West Ham have won just one of their last 10 Premier League games (D4 L5), though it was in their last away match against Manchester United. The Hammers last won consecutive away league games in December 2023.

programme.title: AFC Bournemouth vs Leicester City (MW38)
programme.desc: Bournemouth are unbeaten in all six of their Premier League home games against Leicester (W4 D2), winning the last three by an aggregate score of 10-4.

programme.title: Tottenham Hotspur vs Brighton and Hove Albion (MW38)
programme.desc: Although Spurs are 17th in the Premier League, they've scored more goals (63) than they've conceded (61). The lowest position in a Premier League table a side has finished with a positive goal difference is 16th, by Man City in 2003-04 (+1).

programme.title: Liverpool vs Crystal Palace (MW38)
programme.desc: When finishing the season at home, the Premier League champions have won their final match on 13 out of the last 14 occasions. The only exception was Manchester United losing against West Ham in 2006-07.

programme.title: Southampton vs Arsenal (MW38)
programme.desc: Southampton have lost 29 Premier League games this season. Defeat here would see them become the first team to lose 30 times in a single campaign in the competition's history, while also the first team to do so in a 38-game top-flight season.

programme.title: Nottingham Forest vs Chelsea (MW38)
programme.desc: Nottingham Forest have won 19 Premier League matches this season, more than they did in 2022-23 and 2023-24 combined (18). They last won 20+ games in 1994-95 (22), while they've never done so in a 38-game top-flight season.

programme.title: Wolverhampton Wanderers vs Brentford (MW38)
programme.desc: Brentford have scored 65 Premier League goals this season, their most in a top-flight campaign since 1937-38 (69). Their conversion rate in 2024-25 is 15.2%, the best of any side.

programme.title: Newcastle United vs Everton (MW38)
programme.desc: Victory for Newcastle will guarantee them qualification for next season's UEFA Champions League, the second time in the last three seasons they'll have done so having only done so twice previously (1997-98 and 2002-03).

programme.title: Manchester United vs Aston Villa (MW38)
programme.desc: Manchester United will finish this Premier League campaign without winning two matches in a row all season - it's only the third time in the club's history that they've gone an entire league season without winning back-to-back games.

programme.title: Nottingham Forest vs Chelsea (MW38)
programme.desc: Nottingham Forest have won 19 Premier League matches this season, more than they did in 2022-23 and 2023-24 combined (18). They last won 20+ games in 1994-95 (22), while they've never done so in a 38-game top-flight season.

programme.title: Fulham vs Manchester City (MW38)
programme.desc: Fulham are winless in 19 Premier League meetings with Man City (D3 L16), losing the last 14 in a row. Their last victory was in April 2009 at the Etihad Stadium under Roy Hodgson.

programme.title: Best League 1&2 Moments 1993-2019
programme.desc: All the way from 1993 through to 2019, take a look back over the years and enjoy the very best moments from the League 1 and 2 Play-Off finals.

programme.title: Best Goals: EFL Cup 2022/23
programme.desc: From the Abbey all the way to Wembley - this season's Carabao Cup has thrown up a host of spectacular goals. Look back at the 2022-2023 competition's finest strikes.

programme.title: EFL Trophy Final 2022/23
programme.desc: The moment to shine at Wembley has arrived for Bolton Wanderers and Plymouth Argyle, as the pair turn their attentions away from promotion to the final of the 2022-23 EFL Trophy. (02.

programme.title: EFL 08/09: Cardiff v Swansea
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic clash from the English Football League. Here, Cardiff City meet Swansea City in a thrilling south Wales derby at Ninian Park in 2009.

programme.title: EFL 08/09: Millwall v Scunthorpe
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic clash from the English Football League. Here, Millwall face Scunthorpe at Wembley Stadium in the League 1 play-off final in 2009.

programme.title: EFL 09/10: Sheff Utd v Sheff Weds
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic clash from the English Football League. Here, Sheffield United and Sheffield Wednesday meet in a thrilling Steel City derby in September 2009.

programme.title: EFL 10/11: Peterborough v So'ton
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic clash from the English Football League. Here, Peterborough and Southampton meet in an eight-goal thriller in League 1 in February 2011.

programme.title: EFL Cup 22/23: Man Utd v Newcastle
programme.desc: Twenty four years in the making, Newcastle return to Wembley for a Carabao Cup final with Manchester United, as Erik ten Hag has the chance to end United's six-year trophy drought. (26.

programme.title: EFL Rivalries: East Midlands Derby
programme.desc: A special look at some of the EFL's biggest derby rivalries. Here, the rivalry between East Midlands clubs Nottingham Forest and Derby County, a match with a storied history.

programme.title: EFL Rivalries: East Anglian Derby
programme.desc: A look at some of the great derbies in English football. This episode looks at the 'East Anglian Derby', contested between Norwich City and Ipswich Town.

programme.title: EFL Cup Retro 12: L'pool v Cardiff
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic League Cup final from years gone by. Here, Liverpool and Cardiff City go head-to-head in the final of the 2011-12 competition in a twisting, turning clash.

programme.title: EFL 02/03: Sheff Utd v Wolves
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic English Football League clash. Here, Sheffield United and Wolves meet in the First Division play-off final at the Millennium Stadium in Cardiff back in 2003.

programme.title: EFL 14/15: Brentford v Fulham
programme.desc: Relive some classic action from the English Football League. Here, a west London derby in the Sky Bet Championship between Brentford and Fulham at Griffin Park in November 2014.

programme.title: EFL 14/15: Derby County v N.Forest
programme.desc: Relive some classic English Football League action. Here, a Midlands derby in the Sky Bet Championship between Derby County and Nottingham Forest at the iPro Stadium in January 2015.

programme.title: EFL 14/15: Norwich v Ipswich
programme.desc: A chance to relive some classic action from the EFL. Here, Norwich City and Ipswich Town clash in an East Anglian derby at Carrow Road back in March 2015.

programme.title: EFL 14/15: Wolves v Leeds Utd
programme.desc: A chance to relive some classic action from the EFL. Here, Wolverhampton Wanderers host Leeds United at Molineux in April 2015 in a thrilling game that saw seven goals.

programme.title: Championship Season Review 17/18
programme.desc: Relive all the key moments from the 2017-2018 Sky Bet Championship season, as Wolves charged towards the title and a tight relegation battle ensured at the bottom.

programme.title: Best Goals: EFL Cup: 2022/23
programme.desc: From the Abbey all the way to Wembley - this season's Carabao Cup has thrown up a host of spectacular goals. Look back at the 2022-2023 competition's finest strikes.

programme.title: EFL Trophy Final 2022/23
programme.desc: The moment to shine at Wembley has arrived for Bolton Wanderers and Plymouth Argyle, as the pair turn their attentions away from promotion to the final of the 2022-23 EFL Trophy. (02.

programme.title: EFL League Two Playoff Final
programme.desc: Wembley Stadium sets the stage for AFC Wimbledon and Walsall in the 2024-25 Sky Bet League Two play-off final, with the winner ascending to England's third tier.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: League 1 24/25
programme.desc: Wembley Stadium hosts the Sky Bet League One play-off final, as Charlton Athletic and Leyton Orient go toe to toe for promotion. Charlton emerged victorious in both league games. (25.

programme.title: EFL SF: Leyton Orient v Stockport
programme.desc: Having secured their highest league finish for 23 years, Stockport County visit Brisbane Road for the first leg of their Sky Bet League One play-off semi-final with Leyton Orient. (10.

programme.title: EFL SF: Wycombe v Charlton
programme.desc: The first leg of a Sky Bet League One play-off semi-final. (10.

programme.title: EFL SF: Stockport v Leyton Orient
programme.desc: The second leg of a Sky Bet League One play-off semi-final between Stockport County and Leyton Orient. Things remain all-square after the first leg after a 2-2 thriller. (14.

programme.title: EFL SF: Charlton v Wycombe
programme.desc: The second leg of the Sky Bet League One play-off semi-final between Charlton Athletic and Wycombe Wanderers. With the first leg ending goalless, it is all to play for. (15.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: League 1 24/25
programme.desc: Wembley Stadium hosts the Sky Bet League One play-off final, as Charlton Athletic and Leyton Orient go toe to toe for promotion. Charlton emerged victorious in both league games. (25.

programme.title: Bundesliga: Season Review
programme.desc: A review of the 2024-25 Bundesliga season.

programme.title: EFL PO SF: Bristol City v Sheff U
programme.desc: In the first leg of their Sky Bet Championship play-off semi-final, Bristol City, entering their first play-off since 2008, host third-placed Sheffield United at Ashton Gate. (08.

programme.title: EFL PO SF: Coventry v Sunderland
programme.desc: Aiming to reach their second play-off final in three years, Coventry City face Sunderland at the Coventry Building Society Arena in their Sky Bet Championship semi-final first leg. (09.

programme.title: EFL PO SF: Sheff U v Bristol City
programme.desc: Carrying over a surging 3-0 win over Bristol City last Thursday, Sheffield United return to Bramall Lane to finish the job in their second Sky Bet Championship play-off semi-final. (12.

programme.title: EFL PO SF: Sunderland v Coventry
programme.desc: Bringing the advantage to the Stadium of Light thanks to an Eliezer Mayenda strike, Sunderland face Coventry in the second leg of their Sky Bet Championship play-off semi-final. (13.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: C'Ship 24/25
programme.desc: Sheffield United and Sunderland face off for a place in next year's Premier League, as Wembley Stadium hosts the 2024-25 Sky Bet Championship play-off final. (24.

programme.title: SPFL 24/25 Play-Off 1st Leg
programme.desc: entering their third-play-off final in three years - for a spot in next year's Scottish Premiership in the first leg of the play-off final. (22.

programme.title: SPFL 24/25 Play-Off 2nd Leg
programme.desc: Following a dramatic 1-1 draw on Thursday thanks to Ronan Hale's 96th-minute penalty, Ross County and Livingston meet in the second leg of the Scottish Premiership play-off final. (26.

programme.title: WSL: End of Season Special
programme.desc: As the 2024-25 Women's Super League enters the history books, reflect on another campaign with an end-of-season special.

programme.title: EFL 14/15: Swindon v Sheff Utd
programme.desc: Relive a classic match from the English Football League. Here, Swindon Town host Sheffield United at the County Ground in a 10-goal thriller in the play-offs back in May 2015.

programme.title: PL:Tottenham v Brighton Highlights
programme.desc: After delivering on his promise of a trophy in his second season, Ange Postecoglou returns to a hero's welcome in north London as his Spurs team face Brighton on the final day. (25.

programme.title: PL: Ipswich v West Ham Highlights
programme.desc: Ipswich's Premier League stay comes to an end after just one season, as the Tractor Boys' top-flight campaign concludes with the visit of Graham Potter's West Ham United. (25.

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Leicester Hlts
programme.desc: Andoni Iraola's Bournemouth bring their Premier League campaign to an end with the visit of relegated Leicester. The Cherries' hopes of qualifying for Europe were ended midweek. (25.

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: Forest and Chelsea's Champions League aspirations ride on a crucial game in the race for the top-five. Victory is enough for the Blues while Forest are relying on results elsewhere. (25.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games: Wolves/WBA '12
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the Premier League. Here, Black Country rivals Wolves and West Brom clash at Molineux back in 2012 in a memorable match for the Baggies.

programme.title: PL Greatest: Burnley/Man U 2009
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic Premier League match. Here, Burnley take on Manchester United at Turf Moor in August 2009 on what would be a memorable evening for the Clarets.

programme.title: PL Greatest: Cardiff/Man City 2013
programme.desc: Relive some classic action from the Premier League archive. Here, Cardiff meet Man City on a memorable afternoon in south Wales back in 2013.

programme.title: PL Greatest: Arsenal/Charlton 2001
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the Premier League. Here, Arsenal take on Charlton Athletic at Highbury back in 2001 on a memorable afternoon for the Addicks.

programme.title: Best Premier League Goals 16/17
programme.desc: Sky Sports takes a look at some of the best goals from Premier League seasons in years gone by. Here, a look at the best goals scored in the 2016-17 campaign.

programme.title: Best PL Goals: Chelsea
programme.desc: Enjoy a selection of the very best goals scored in the Premier League by Chelsea, including memorable strikes from Didier Drogba, Frank Lampard, Eden Hazard and others.

programme.title: PL 100 Club: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: A celebration of some of the finest goalscorers in Premier League history. This episode features the best of Thierry Henry's 175 top-flight goals.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:M City/L'Pool 15
programme.desc: Relive a Premier League classic, as Man City face Liverpool back in November 2015. City needed a win to go back top of the table, while Liverpool were adapting to life under Jurgen Klopp.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:M City/L'Pool 13
programme.desc: Relive a Premier League classic, as Man City face Liverpool back in 2013. City needed three points to close the gap at the top to Man Utd, while the Reds were chasing a top four spot.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man U/L'Pool '95
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Man United host Liverpool in October 1995, as Eric Cantona returned from his lengthy suspension in a thrilling 2-2 draw.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:L'Pool/Man U '97
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Liverpool meet Manchester United at Anfield in 1997, in a match that had a happy outcome for the Red Devils.

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: Forest and Chelsea's Champions League aspirations ride on a crucial game in the race for the top-five. Victory is enough for the Blues while Forest are relying on results elsewhere. (25.

programme.title: PL:Liverpool v Crystal Palace Hlts
programme.desc: On the final day of the Premier League, Liverpool's crowning moment arrives against FA Cup winners Crystal Palace at Anfield as Arne Slot's Reds are presented with the trophy. (25.

programme.title: PL: Southampton v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: Second-placed Arsenal go to already-relegated Southampton on the final day of the Premier League season. William Saliba misses the Gunners' trip to St Mary's due to injury. (25.

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Manchester City Hlts
programme.desc: A return to Europe's top table remains the aim for Manchester City in an otherwise disappointing Premier League campaign, as Pep Guardiola's men go to Fulham on the final day. (21.

programme.title: PL: Newcastle v Everton Highlights
programme.desc: The St James' Park faithful ready themselves for the final top-five push as Newcastle take on Everton knowing victory secures the club Champions League football for next season. (25.

programme.desc: A place in Europe next season is still a possibility for Brentford as Thomas Frank takes his side to Molineux for a final day Premier League clash with VÃ­tor Pereira's Wolves. (25.
